concepts.title: Position of point wrt circle
concepts.conceptBody:
Position of a point with respect to circle 

Let the equation of the circle be $S\equiv {{x}^{2}}+{{y}^{2}}+2gx+2fy+c=0$ and $A\left( {{x}_{1}},{{y}_{1}} \right)$ be any point.

Distance between the Centre $\left( -g,-f \right)$ and $A$ is given by $d=\sqrt{{{\left( {{x}_{1}}+g \right)}^{2}}+{{\left( {{y}_{1}}+f \right)}^{2}}}$

${{\text{d}}^{2}}-{{\text{r}}^{2}}=\text{x}_{1}^{2}+\text{y}_{1}^{2}+2\text{g}{{\text{x}}_{1}}+2\text{f}{{\text{y}}_{1}}+{{\text{g}}^{2}}+{{\text{f}}^{2}}-{{\text{g}}^{2}}-{{\text{f}}^{2}}+\text{c}=\text{x}_{1}^{2}+\text{y}_{1}^{2}+2\text{g}{{\text{x}}_{1}}+2\text{f}{{\text{y}}_{1}}+\text{c}={{\text{S}}_{1}}$

Clearly A lies inside, on or outside the circle as $\text{d}<$, =, or $>$ i.e., ${{\text{d}}^{2}}-{{\text{r}}^{2}}<,=$, or $<0$

Hence

${{\text{S}}_{1}}\left( \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ A} \right)<0\Rightarrow $ point lies inside the circle.

${{\text{S}}_{1}}\left( \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ A} \right)=0\Rightarrow $ point lies on the circle.

${{\text{S}}_{1}}\left( \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ A} \right)>0\Rightarrow $ point lies outside the circle.

Maximum and Minimum Distance of a Point from the Circle 

Let any point $\text{P}\left( {{\text{x}}_{1}},{{\text{y}}_{1}} \right)$ and circle

$S\equiv {{x}^{2}}+{{y}^{2}}+2gx+2fy+c=0$ … (1)

The Centre and radius of the circle are

$\text{C}\left( -\text{g},-\text{f} \right)\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ and }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\sqrt{\left( {{\text{g}}^{2}}+{{\text{f}}^{2}}-\text{c} \right)}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ respectively }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }$

Case I: 

If $\mathbf{P}$ inside the circle

 

In this case ${{\text{S}}_{1}}<0$

$\therefore \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ r}=\sqrt{\left( {{\text{g}}^{2}}+{{\text{f}}^{2}}-\text{c} \right)}=\text{CA}=\text{CB}$

The minimum distance of $\text{P}$ from circle

$\begin{array}{*{35}{r}} {} & ~=\text{PA}=\text{CA}-\text{CP} \\ {} & ~=\left| \text{r}-\text{CP} \right| \\ \end{array}$

and the maximum distance of $\text{P}$ from circle $=\text{PB}$

$=\text{CB}+\text{CP}=\left| \text{r}+\text{CP} \right|$

Case II: 

If $\mathbf{P}$ outside the circle

In this case ${{\text{S}}_{1}}>0$

the minimum distance of $\text{P}$ from circle

$=\text{PA}=\text{CP}-\text{CA}=\left| \text{CP}-\text{r} \right|$

and the maximum distance of $\text{P}$ from the circle

$=\text{PB}=\text{CP}+\text{CB}=\left| \text{r}+\text{CP} \right|$

 

Case III: 

If $P$ on the circle

In this case ${{S}_{1}}=0$

the minimum distance of $\text{P}$ from the circle $=0$

and the maximum distance of $\text{P}$ from the circle $=\text{PA}=2\text{r}$
